Oil Prices Fall as Trump Launches “Project Freedom” and OPEC+ Increases Output
headlines.summary: Oil prices edged lower in early Asian trading on Monday after President Trump announced the U.S. would guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz and OPEC+ confirmed a modest output increase. At the time of writing, WTI crude was down 0.65% at $101.30, while Brent crude had dropped

headlines.title: Big Oil Begins Return to Canada amid Energy Crunch
headlines.summary: For about ten years, Big Oil moved away from Canada’s oil sands and into cheaper, more easily developed—and less regulated—locations. Now, the supermajors are returning, looking for a piece of an energy industry that just got a lot more attractive. Last week, Shell said it would

headlines.title: China Flips the Switch on Fuel Exports as Asia Runs Short
headlines.summary: Beijing is reversing its curbs on refined fuel exports after halting shipments in the opening days of the U.S.-Iran conflict. headlines.title: Russia’s Oil Revenues Surge as the World Scrambles for Supply
headlines.summary: Following the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, several major world powers introduced strict sanctions on trade with Moscow.
